---
title: "Replication: Bloom (2009)"
subtitle: "The Impact of Uncertainty Shocks"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

## Overview

This document replicates the main empirical results of @bloom2009, which identifies uncertainty shocks using a Cholesky decomposition. The uncertainty indicator — constructed from 17 identified high-volatility events — is ordered first so that it has a contemporaneous effect on all other variables but is itself unaffected by them within the month.


## Setup

```{r}
#| label: setup
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyMacro)
library(tictoc)

set_theme(ftheme_tidyMacro())
```

---

## Data

```{r}
#| label: data
data("Bloom2009")

dates_vec <- Bloom2009 |> pull(Date)
y         <- Bloom2009 |> select(-Date) |> as.matrix()

T <- nrow(y)
N <- ncol(y)

var_names <- colnames(y)
shockname <- "UNCERT"
shock     <- match(shockname, var_names)
```

---

## VAR Estimation

```{r}
#| label: var-estimation

p <- 12   # lag length used in the original paper
c <- 1

var_bloom <- fVAR(y, p, c)
sigma     <- var_bloom$sigma

# Cholesky factor (lower triangular)
S <- t(chol(sigma))

# Wold IRFs
horizon   <- 48
wold      <- fwoldIRF(var_bloom, horizon = horizon)
point_irf <- fcholeskyIRF(wold, S)
```

---

## Bootstrap Confidence Bands

### Standard Bootstrap

```{r}
#| label: bootstrap-standard
tic()
bloom_chol <- fbootstrapChol(
    y          = y,
    var_result = var_bloom,
    nboot      = 1000,
    horizon    = horizon,
    bootscheme = "residual",
    n_threads  = 3
)
toc()
```

### Bias-Corrected Bootstrap

```{r}
#| label: bootstrap-corrected

tic()
bloom_corrected <- fbootstrapCholCorrected(
    y          = y,
    var_result = var_bloom,
    nboot1     = 1000,
    nboot2     = 2000,
    horizon    = horizon,
    bootscheme = "residual",
    n_threads  = 4
)
toc()
```

---

## Impulse Response Functions

### Standard Bootstrap

```{r}
#| label: fig-irf-standard
#| fig-cap: "IRFs to uncertainty shock — standard bootstrap (90%/68% confidence bands)"

fplotirf_chol(
    point       = point_irf,
    boot_result = bloom_chol,
    shock       = shock,
    varnames    = var_names,
    facet_ncol  = 4
)
```

### Bias-Corrected Bootstrap

```{r}
#| label: fig-irf-corrected
#| fig-cap: "IRFs to uncertainty shock — bias-corrected bootstrap (90%/68% confidence bands)"

fplotirf_chol(
    point       = point_irf,
    boot_result = bloom_corrected,
    shock       = shock,
    varnames    = var_names,
    facet_ncol  = 4
)
```

---

## Forecast Error Variance Decomposition

```{r}
#| label: fig-fevd
#| fig-cap: "Forecast error variance decomposition: share explained by uncertainty shock"

vardec <- fevd_chol(point_irf, shock = shock)

fplot_vardec(
    fevd       = vardec$fevd,
    varnames   = var_names,
    shocknames = shockname
) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = tidyMacro_colors[c(2, 3)])
```

---

## Historical Decomposition

```{r}
#| label: fig-histdec
#| fig-cap: "Historical decomposition: contribution of uncertainty shock"

# Exclude the shock variable itself from the response variables
series <- setdiff(seq_len(N), shock)

histdec_list <- setNames(
    lapply(series, function(i) fhistdec(y, var_bloom, S, i)$histdec),
    colnames(y)[series]
)

fplot_histdec(
    histdec_list = histdec_list,
    shock        = shock,
    shockname    = shockname,
    dates        = dates_vec,
    p            = p,
    facet_ncol   = 2
) +
    scale_x_date(date_breaks = "7 years", date_labels = "%Y")
```
